title: [lichen_l2.c:117] LICHEN_MIN_FRAME_LEN assumes minimum MIC size
description:
Gap found: Frame size constant derivation not verified against all MIC sizes.

Why this was missed: MIC size variability not considered in Round 1.

The actual bug: LICHEN_MIN_FRAME_LEN (line 117) is set to 9 bytes, calculated using LICHEN_MIC_32_LEN (4 bytes) as the minimum MIC. The comment explains this is for 'broadcast, 32-bit MIC, empty payload'.

The frame format (spec section 4) supports different MIC sizes:
- 32-bit MIC: 4 bytes
- 64-bit MIC: 8 bytes

If the protocol ever REQUIRES 64-bit MIC minimum, LICHEN_MIN_FRAME_LEN would need to increase. The BUILD_ASSERT at lines 137-144 validates the current calculation.

The actual issue: if lichen_link_rx() requires a larger MIC for certain security levels and the frame only has a 32-bit MIC, the frame would be accepted for length checking but fail MIC verification. This is correct behavior - the length check is a fast early-reject, not the final validation.

Verified correct. Priority 4 - design documentation.
close_reason: Fixed: Added MIC size clarification comment explaining that 32-bit MIC is the minimum, 64-bit MIC frames are always longer, and that MIC size/security policy enforcement happens during lichen_link_rx() MIC verification, not at this early length check.
